text.title: Agent Context-Engineering Corpus Standard Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Agent Context-Engineering Corpus Standard application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Use the closed term set: context window, working memory, episodic/semantic memory, tool/system/transient/persistent context, eviction, compaction, citation, anchor, prefix, suffix. New terms require glossary PR.
rule.rationale: Term sprawl is the #1 cause of redundant entries; closed glossary forces deduplication.

rule.statement: Every claim cites ≥3 sources: ≥1 primary (Anthropic/OpenAI/Google paper or doc), ≥1 secondary (engineer post/talk), ≥1 production (code/eval reference).
rule.rationale: Anti-hallucination + anti-vibes: corpus must be falsifiable + trace-able.

rule.statement: Artefact: AGENTS.md + content/01-core-rules.xml + content/02-output-contract.xml + content/03-failure-modes.xml. Deviations require corpus-standard amendment first.
rule.rationale: Uniform shape makes corpus searchable + embeddable for retrieval.

rule.statement: Patterns first, vendor mappings second. 'Claude does X, OpenAI Y, Gemini Z' is OK; 'do X in Claude' as only framing is not.
rule.rationale: Vendor-locked corpus dies when models change; pattern-first survives across providers.

rule.statement: Every entry has last_reviewed date. After 12 months without review, entry shows 'stale' badge until re-validated or superseded.
rule.rationale: Anti-decay: AI-context patterns shift 6-18 months; explicit decay rule beats silent rot.
